title: Rule
description: A governance rule as a first-class, versioned object. Replaces rules-as-prose-in-markdown. Two independent axes: lifecycleStatus is where the rule sits in the maturity pipeline (it can hold there); tier is which gate mode is firing at runtime (it changes on use/state, independent of lifecycleStatus). A rule carries gate actions for every tier it may occupy and traverses tiers via defined transitions. The schema holds logical state; git history holds the proof of how it got there (see refinementRecord). Rule instances live at schemas/_instances/rules/RULE-<DOMAIN>-<TOPIC>-N.json — canonical instance namespace established in d-2026-05-31-deliverable-schema-foundation.

$schema.description: JSON Schema declaration for instance files referencing this schema.

id.description: Stable identifier. Convention: RULE-<DOMAIN>-<TOPIC>-N. Matches the registry id-pattern style (cf. ^FU-..., ^d-...). Example: RULE-PIPELINE-PROJECTION-CONTRACT-1.

version.description: Semver of the rule object. MAJOR: gate-action change that can break work. MINOR: added tier capability or relaxed transition. PATCH: clarification, no behavior change.

statement.description: The rule itself, in one or two sentences. The prose that used to live in a markdown file now lives here as a field.

rationale.description: Why the rule exists — often the failure that produced it. Optional but encouraged for rules lifted from working-model.

authority.description: Governance authority block — who owns the rule object itself. Distinct from gateActions.human-loop.authority which is RUNTIME authority (who fires the gate at execution). Rule-level authority is GOVERNANCE authority: who authored the rule object, who approves lifecycleStatus transitions, who can revert. Currently free-form strings; seeds the 5-tier authorization model (FU-RULE-SCHEMA-AUTHORITY-TIER-ENUM-1). Matches deliverable.schema.json authority shape exactly so the auth-tier enum promotion updates both schemas in one PR.

approver.description: Who holds approval authority for lifecycleStatus transitions (typically Erik L4 in current portfolio).

revertAuthority.description: Who can revert the rule (transition lifecycleStatus to reverted).

lifecycleStatus.description: AXIS 1 — maturity pipeline. Where the rule sits; it can hold here independent of tier. working-model: proven under load, not yet schema-bound enforcement. formalized: bound and enforced. deprecated: superseded, retained for trace. reverted: rolled back after proving wrong.

tierState.description: AXIS 2 — runtime gate mode. Independent of lifecycleStatus. The tier changes based on use/state; it is not the rule's maturity.

activeTier.description: The tier whose gate action is firing right now.

occupiableTiers.description: Every tier this rule is allowed to occupy.

terminalTier.description: The highest-value tier this rule can ultimately reach. 'automated' is the highest state of value. A rule whose nature requires judgment declares terminalTier 'human-loop' — correct by design, not incomplete.

gateActions.description: What the rule does at each tier it can occupy. Every rule carries the full set for its occupiable tiers; activeTier selects which fires.

human-loop.description: The SME-gated action. A legitimate, permanent terminal for judgment-based rules; improvable but may never automate.

gate.description: What the human is asked to decide or approve.

automationPath.description: If a path to automate exists, name it. If none exists by nature, state that — so the terminal is a decision, not a gap.

automated.description: The auto-execute action. Terminates as a real CI check — integrity is mechanical, no trust required.

predicate.description: The condition checked, stated precisely enough to implement.

ciSurface.description: Where the check lives and runs — the repo-versioned, branch-delivered enforcement point.

checkRef.description: Optional pointer to the check implementation (path, job name, or script).

onFail.description: block: fail the build. warn: annotate, pass. escalate: route to human-loop tier.

refine.description: The refinement action, available at any tier. Refinement happens in a branch and lands via PR; the commit is the audit record.

action.description: What refining this rule means in practice.

transitions.description: State transitions — covers both tier-axis promotions (working-model → human-loop → automated) and lifecycleStatus-axis promotions (working-model → formalized). Each transition names from-state, to-state, and trigger. evidenceClass required on promotion transitions (to formalized or automated); not required on downgrades (to working-model, deprecated, reverted).

from.description: Prior state (tier or lifecycleStatus) before this transition.

to.description: Target state (tier or lifecycleStatus) after this transition.

trigger.description: The condition that fires the transition.

evidenceClass.description: Why this promotion is warranted — required when promoting to 'formalized' or 'automated'. Promotions UP the maturity ladder must declare their evidence basis. Free-form string in v1.1; will become an enum in v1.2 when patterns crystallize across 3+ instances (FU-RULE-SCHEMA-EVIDENCECLASS-ENUM-1). Downgrade transitions (to working-model, deprecated, reverted) do not require evidenceClass.

gatingBinding.description: Where the rule attaches to the branching flow. Rules may arrive after a branch is cut to gate that branch's CI in a single pass — so a branch tests both the work and the rule. Follows the associative-reference pattern (cf. relatedDecisions).

branchPattern.description: Branch name pattern this rule gates. Example: 'chore/*', 'fix/*', '*'.

gatedWork.description: What work this rule gates. Example: 'any PR touching .roadmap/*.json'.

ciSurface.description: The CI surface the binding attaches to, if different from gateActions.automated.ciSurface.

refinementRecord.description: Version history. Reuses the resolution-object precedent (commitSha, prUrl) from follow-up-register. The schema holds logical state; each entry points at the commit that made the change, so git holds the proof. This is the rule's audit trail through the branching flow.

commitSha.description: Commit SHA that landed this version of the rule. Reuses follow-up-register resolution.commitSha naming.

prUrl.description: PR that landed the change. Reuses follow-up-register resolution.prUrl naming.

date.description: Wall-clock date of the change (system time at authoring).

supersededBy.description: Rule id that replaces this one, if any. Reuses decision-log supersededBy naming.

tier.description: Runtime gate mode (AXIS 2). working-model: voluntary pre-enforcement mode. human-loop: SME-gated, judgment-based, legitimate terminal. automated: real CI check, mechanical enforcement, highest state of value.

ruleState.description: Combined state value for rule transitions — covers both tier axis (working-model, human-loop, automated) and lifecycleStatus axis (working-model, formalized, deprecated, reverted). Used in transitions.items so a single transitions array captures both tier promotions and lifecycle promotions.
